rashri.bioShort: It's not just about designing, it's about building identities.
rashri.bio: I am a designer passionate about giving visual form to ideas and turning them into experiences that connect emotionally with people. I specialize in creating worlds with their own unique identity, where every artistic detail serves a narrative purpose. At Rhon Studios, my goal is to ensure every pixel tells a story and every game feels like a universe worth exploring.

camilo.bioShort: I don't just develop, I create stories.
camilo.bio: I am a game developer passionate about creating interactive experiences and immersive gameplay systems. I have experience with Unreal Engine, Unity, and Godot, as well as multiplayer, virtual reality (VR), and both 2D and 3D game development. My goal is to create memorable games that deliver unique experiences to players.

angelramirez.tagline: Systems engineer building tools and video games where engineering and design work together.
angelramirez.bio: Systems Engineer specializing in observability. I currently design monitoring solutions using Grafana and OpenTelemetry while exploring game development with Godot as a personal project. I'm passionate about creating tools and experiences where engineering and design complement each other.

sayyidali.bio: I work on the full development pipeline, from modelling, texturing, and animating in Blender to assembling, wiring and programming in game engines. I especially love modelling architecture, towns, cities, putting together levels, and programming systems that make worlds feel alive and interactive.

tinomhedziso.tagline: Be safe, friend. Don't you dare go hollow.
tinomhedziso.bio:
Web developer by trade, professional overthinker, and lifelong learner. I enjoy the technical side of things, improving performance, refining user experiences, and making sure the small details don't get overlooked, but what keeps me excited about any project is the people behind it.
Rhon Studios is more than a collection of games. It's a group of ambitious people building something they genuinely believe in. The games are important, but for me the best part of the journey is the conversations, the ideas, the lessons learned, and the people I get to build alongside.
